basic_details.job_title: BEL Bharat Electronics Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 09 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) has invited online applications for 9 posts of Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) at its Chennai Unit through Advertisement No. 4788/002/REC/HR&A/CHN/2026 dated 17 June 2026. Candidates with a B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engineering degree in Mechanical Engineering, aged up to 28 years as on 01 June 2026, can apply online until 01 July 2026. Selection is through a 100-mark written test, and selected candidates start at Rs. 30,000 per month.

application_fee.details: Candidates wondering about the cost of applying can note that General, EWS, and OBC candidates are required to pay Rs. 177 (Rs. 150 application fee plus 18% GST) through the SBI Collect payment gateway. SC, ST, and PwBD candidates are fully exempted from paying this application fee. The fee, once paid, is non-refundable under any circumstance, and applications submitted without the prescribed fee (where applicable) will not be considered.

age_limit.details: Candidates asking who can apply for BEL Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) should know that a B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engineering (4-year course) in Mechanical Engineering with pass class from a recognized University or Institution is the minimum requirement, and no post-qualification work experience is needed. Only the Mechanical Engineering branch is treated as relevant for this post, and if the specialization printed on a candidate's degree certificate does not match the branch selected in the online application, that application will not be considered for selection. The upper age limit is 28 years as on 01 June 2026, with relaxations available for reserved categories. | Educational qualification requirements include: | B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engg in Mechanical Engineering (4-year course), pass class, from a recognized university. Candidates must possess all semester/year mark sheets along with the final degree certificate. A CGPA-to-percentage conversion certificate is required wherever the marksheet uses CGPA grading. | Age relaxation as on 01 June 2026: | ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (effective limit: 33 years). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (effective limit: 31 years). PwBD candidates with 40% or more disability get an additional 10 years relaxation on top of the relaxation applicable to their category. SSLC/SSC/ISC marks card or any other valid document is accepted as proof of date of birth.

qualification.details: Candidates asking who can apply for BEL Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) should know that a B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engineering (4-year course) in Mechanical Engineering with pass class from a recognized University or Institution is the minimum requirement, and no post-qualification work experience is needed. Only the Mechanical Engineering branch is treated as relevant for this post, and if the specialization printed on a candidate's degree certificate does not match the branch selected in the online application, that application will not be considered for selection. The upper age limit is 28 years as on 01 June 2026, with relaxations available for reserved categories. | Educational qualification requirements include: | B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engg in Mechanical Engineering (4-year course), pass class, from a recognized university. Candidates must possess all semester/year mark sheets along with the final degree certificate. A CGPA-to-percentage conversion certificate is required wherever the marksheet uses CGPA grading. | Age relaxation as on 01 June 2026: | ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (effective limit: 33 years). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (effective limit: 31 years). PwBD candidates with 40% or more disability get an additional 10 years relaxation on top of the relaxation applicable to their category. SSLC/SSC/ISC marks card or any other valid document is accepted as proof of date of birth.

salary_details.details: Candidates asking about the pay for Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) can note that BEL offers a consolidated monthly remuneration of Rs. 30,000 in the 1st year, Rs. 35,000 in the 2nd year, and Rs. 40,000 in the 3rd year if the contract is extended. In addition, BEL pays a consolidated Rs. 12,000 per year toward insurance premium, attire allowance, stitching charges, and footwear allowance, released in two installments - the first with the first month's salary and the second after completing 6 months of service. | Engagement terms include: | Initial engagement period: 2 years, extendable by up to 1 year (maximum tenure of 3 years) based on project requirement and performance. Insurance coverage of Rs. 2 lakh for medical cover and Rs. 5 lakh for life cover must be arranged by the Trainee Engineer at the time of joining, with the policy copy submitted to HR; renewal and premium payment remain the individual's responsibility throughout the tenure. | Advancement to Project Engineer-I: After completing 2 years as Trainee Engineer, candidates become eligible for advancement to Project Engineer-I if they score 80% or above (aggregate) in Performance Assessment for both years, maintain attendance above 80% in the preceding year with good general discipline, and have no adverse remarks in the preceding 2 years. Candidates who do not meet these criteria continue as Trainee Engineer-III for one additional year, after which the engagement ends. Once advanced, Project Engineer-I is engaged for 3 years, extendable by up to 1 year based on project requirement and performance. | Retention Bonus for Project Engineers: Project Engineers who have advanced from the Trainee Engineer role are entitled to a Retention Bonus of Rs. 25,000 for every completed year, provided they complete an overall tenure of 4 years in the Project Engineer role. This bonus is calculated for the Project Engineer tenure only and is paid as a lump sum of Rs. 1,00,000 at the end of 4 years.

selection_process: Candidates asking how they will be selected can note that BEL shortlists eligible applicants for a written test carrying 100 marks
and final selection follows merit order calculated category-wise and discipline-wise. The minimum qualifying marks differ by category: General
while ST and PwBD candidates need 30% to be considered suitable. The question paper carries negative marking of 1/4 mark for every wrong response
so candidates should attempt questions carefully
and the written test will be conducted in Chennai. Separately

the notification mentions that candidates currently working on a permanent or fixed-tenure basis in PSUs or Government organizations must produce a No Objection Certificate "at the time of the interview
" which indicates there may be a verification or interview stage for such candidates after the written test.
As a general industry pattern (not stated in the official notification)
PSU recruitment of this kind typically takes 6–10 weeks from the application closing date to the written test
followed by another 4–6 weeks for results and document verification. Candidates should treat this only as a rough planning estimate and rely on the official BEL website for the actual schedule.
how_to_apply: Candidates ready to apply should know that applications are accepted only through the online mode, and no manual, paper, or hard copy applications will be entertained under any circumstances. Before starting the form, candidates should keep all required documents, photographs, and the application fee payment ready, since changes are not permitted once the form is submitted.
Read the eligibility criteria and confirm you meet the qualification, branch, and age requirements.
Fill in all details accurately in the online application form, since edits are not allowed post-submission.
Pay the application fee of Rs. 177 via the SBI Collect link (skip this step if you belong to SC/ST/PwBD).
Note down the SBI Collect Reference Number generated after payment and enter it in the application form.
Upload all mandatory documents, including educational certificates, category certificates, and ID proof.
Verify the entire form carefully and submit it before 01 July 2026.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for BEL Trainee Engineer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window closes on 01 July 2026. No applications will be accepted after this date.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical)?
faq.answer: There are 9 total vacancies, split as UR-3, EWS-2, OBC-3, and ST-1.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required?
faq.answer: Candidates need a B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engineering (4-year course) in Mechanical Engineering with pass class from a recognized university; no work experience is required.

faq.question: Q4. What is the upper age limit for this post?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 28 years as on 01 June 2026, with relaxation of 5 years for ST, 3 years for OBC, and an additional 10 years for PwBD candidates with 40% or more disability.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for BEL Trainee Engineer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: General, EWS, and OBC candidates pay Rs. 177, while SC, ST, and PwBD candidates are exempted from the fee.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Selection is based on a 100-mark written test held in Chennai, with qualifying marks of 35% for General/EWS/OBC-NCL and 30% for ST/PwBD, and negative marking of 1/4 mark per wrong answer.

faq.question: Q7. What salary will a Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) receive?
faq.answer: The pay is Rs. 30,000 per month in year 1, Rs. 35,000 in year 2, and Rs. 40,000 in year 3 (if extended), plus a consolidated Rs. 12,000 per year for allowances.
